meta.defaultDescription: The PlayAble Network — building pathways for youth development through sport, mentorship, and community engagement.

home.heroDescription: The PlayAble Network empowers children and young people in underserved communities through sport, mentorship, and community-driven programmes that create opportunities for growth, inclusion, and positive change.
home.missionTitle: Every child deserves the chance to play, learn, grow, and thrive.
home.missionBody: Through sport, mentorship, and community engagement, The PlayAble Network creates pathways for young people in underserved communities to develop their potential and build stronger futures.

pillars.title: Sport for Development
pillars.blurb: Using sport as a tool to build life skills, confidence, discipline, teamwork, leadership, and social inclusion.

pillars.title: Education & Mentorship
pillars.blurb: Providing learning opportunities, guidance, and personal development programmes that help young people reach their full potential.

pillars.title: Community Impact
pillars.blurb: Creating safe, inclusive, and sustainable initiatives that strengthen communities and improve the wellbeing of children and families.

about.title: About Us
about.tagline: Building pathways for youth development through sport, mentorship, and community engagement.
about.intro: The PlayAble Network is a youth development initiative that uses sport as a platform for engagement, mentorship, learning, and positive social development.
about.storyBody:
Established from a belief that sport can provide more than recreation, The PlayAble Network works alongside communities, families, schools, grassroots organisations, and local partners to create structured opportunities for young people to learn, develop, and participate positively within their communities.

Our programmes combine sport, mentorship, safeguarding, and community engagement to create supportive environments where young people can build confidence, strengthen social connections, develop life skills, and pursue positive pathways for growth.

The PlayAble Network was established to demonstrate how structured sport programmes can strengthen youth participation, community engagement, mentorship, and local capacity development while creating opportunities that extend beyond the field of play.
about.visionBody: To see young people empowered through sport, supported by their communities, and equipped to realise their potential.
about.missionBody: To use sport, mentorship, and community engagement as practical platforms for youth development, learning, and positive participation.
about.teamIntro: The people running the work on the ground every day.

teamMembers.name: Racheal Claire Wachira
teamMembers.bio: Provides strategic leadership, organisational oversight, stakeholder engagement, partnerships, and programme coordination.

teamMembers.name: Remy Sheikh
teamMembers.bio: Leads programme design, implementation, mentorship integration, and football-based youth development activities.

teamMembers.name: Ray Mwaniki
teamMembers.bio: Provides technical football guidance, coach support, player development oversight, and grassroots football expertise.

teamMembers.name: Juliana Angela
teamMembers.bio: Supports community mobilisation, parent engagement, stakeholder relations, and partnership development.

programs.title: Our Programmes
programs.intro: The PlayAble Network uses football as an entry point for youth development, coach development, and community engagement.

areas.title: Youth Football Programmes
areas.body: Structured football activities that encourage participation, teamwork, discipline, confidence, and personal development.

areas.title: Mentorship and Life Skills
areas.body: Positive role models and learning opportunities that support character development, decision-making, leadership, and resilience.

areas.title: Coach Development
areas.body: Supporting grassroots coaches through training, mentorship, safeguarding awareness, and youth-centred coaching approaches.

areas.title: Community Engagement
areas.body: Working with parents, guardians, community leaders, schools, and local partners to ground programmes in community needs.

areas.title: Safeguarding & Participant Wellbeing
areas.body: Promoting safe, inclusive, and supportive environments through clear safeguarding practices and participant protection measures.

areas.title: Partnership Development
areas.body: Collaborating with organisations, institutions, and community stakeholders to expand opportunities for young people.

programs.modelIntro: Football is the entry point. Each stage builds on the last to create lasting community impact.

modelSteps.title: Youth Participation
modelSteps.body: Structured football and mentorship activities that provide young people with positive opportunities for learning, growth, and participation.

modelSteps.title: Coach Development
modelSteps.body: Training and supporting grassroots coaches to strengthen local capacity, safeguarding awareness, and youth-centred coaching approaches.

modelSteps.title: Community Competitions
modelSteps.body: Structured opportunities for participation through football festivals, tournaments, and community-based competition pathways.

modelSteps.title: Partnerships & Opportunities
modelSteps.body: Collaborations that expand opportunities for young people while strengthening the wider football ecosystem.

modelSteps.title: Community Impact
modelSteps.body: Strengthening local capacity and creating lasting positive change in the communities we serve.

programs.visionBody: A network of community-based football programmes where young people progress through structured participation, coaches are strengthened, and communities take active ownership of youth development through sport.

impact.title: Impact & Stories
impact.intro: Real programmes, real communities, real young people. Every initiative is grounded in local partnership and measured by participation, safeguarding, and lasting community ownership.

programmes.name: Community Youth Football & Mentorship Programme
programmes.summary: A community-based football and youth development programme designed and delivered with Gators Academy, focused on vulnerable and underserved young people.
programmes.body: Beginning with a small group of participants, the initiative grew to support 42 boys and girls across multiple age categories. Activities were delivered throughout the week and provided a structured environment where young people could learn, interact, and participate in positive community-based activities while developing teamwork, discipline, and personal responsibility.

programmes.name: Inclusive Youth Football Pilot
programmes.summary: An inclusive youth football pilot delivered with Hill Side FC, combining football, mentorship, community engagement, and safeguarding-focused activities.
programmes.body: The pilot tested a community-based football and mentorship model centred on local partnerships, parent engagement, and participant wellbeing. Thirty-six boys aged 10–16 years participated in structured football sessions and mentorship activities, with parents and guardians engaged from the outset.

partners.title: Partnerships
partners.intro: The PlayAble Network works with local organisations, coaches, parents, and community stakeholders to support programme delivery and strengthen youth development initiatives.

list.name: Gators Academy
list.description: Community partner supporting football, mentorship, and youth engagement in Mombasa.

list.name: Hill Side FC
list.description: Implementation partner supporting local programme delivery, coaching, and community engagement in Turkana.

partners.footer: Our programmes are further strengthened through the active involvement of parents, guardians, coaches, and community leaders. If you'd like to partner with us, get in touch.

governance.title: Governance & Compliance
governance.intro: Safe participation is fundamental to all PlayAble Network activities and partnerships. Safeguarding is integrated into programme planning, delivery, monitoring, and reporting.
governance.safeguardingFooter: Safeguarding considerations are integrated into programme planning, delivery, monitoring, and reporting, and remain a core component of all activities.
governance.complianceBody: NGO registration and compliance documentation is being finalised and will be published here once complete. Partner organisations conducting due diligence can request the latest documentation via email.

support.title: Support Our Work
support.intro: Every contribution helps create more opportunities for young people to play, learn, and grow. There are several ways to support The PlayAble Network.

ways.title: Donate
ways.body: Direct financial support that goes into programme delivery, equipment, safeguarding training, and capacity-building. Reach out for current ways to give.

ways.title: Partner With Us
ways.body: Strategic partnerships with foundations, businesses, sports clubs, and like-minded organisations. We're always looking for collaborators who share our mission.

ways.title: Volunteer
ways.body: Mentor, coach, or contribute professional skills to programmes on the ground. Especially welcome: coach educators, safeguarding professionals, and communications specialists.

support.contactBody: Reach out to discuss how you'd like to support our work — every conversation starts with a message.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: We'd love to hear from you — for partnerships, programme enquiries, or any way to support the work.
contact.formSuccess: Thanks — we'll be in touch shortly.
contact.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly.
